title: Code-index CLI is parity-guarded, not auto-propagating
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/code-index-cli-manifest.ts:10-21 and :23-35 hardcode EXPECTED_TOOL_NAMES and throw on unexpected schema names; .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/027-xce-research-based-refinement/016-cli-tooling-ux/spec.md:72 claims shared registries mean new tools auto-propagate.
detail: The current CLI list is in sync with CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS, but the implementation intentionally rejects any schema count/name change until EXPECTED_TOOL_NAMES is updated. That contradicts the later docs claim that new code-index tools auto-propagate from the shared registry.
fix_sketch: Either derive the manifest acceptance list entirely from CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS or reword docs to say code-index is registry-backed with an explicit parity guard requiring manual acceptance for new tools.

title: Dispatcher still has an independent hardcoded tool registry
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/index.ts:80-86 lists tools from CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS but dispatches separately; .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tools/code-graph-tools.ts:21-31 hardcodes TOOL_NAMES and :84-120 hardcodes the switch; .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/SKILL.md:303 and :333 forbid hardcoded tool lists in router/caller code.
detail: CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS and TOOL_DEFINITIONS are a true alias pair, and the CLI definitions reference the schema array directly. The remaining drift risk is the MCP dispatcher: a future schema-only addition can be advertised by ListTools while missing from TOOL_NAMES/switch until separate code is updated.
fix_sketch: Introduce one registration table pairing each ToolDefinition with its handler, then generate CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS, TOOL_NAMES, dispatch, and CLI manifest from that table.
